name: Turbonomic Application Resource Management API
what_it_does: AI-powered Application Resource Management (ARM) platform with REST API — continuously analyzing application performance and infrastructure capacity to autonomously recommend or execute resource actions (scale, move, resize) across cloud, on-premises, and Kubernetes environments to optimize cost and performance simultaneously.
use_cases: Querying resource optimization actions and savings recommendations via API
Automating resource rightsizing decisions based on Turbonomic ML analysis
Integrating Turbonomic action recommendations with change management workflows
Monitoring application performance assurance metrics across hybrid cloud via API
Pulling cloud cost savings realized from executed optimizations for FinOps reporting
not_for: Simple cloud cost visibility without automation (use CloudHealth or Vantage)
Application performance monitoring without resource management (use Datadog or Dynatrace)
Small organizations without hybrid cloud or complex infrastructure
best_when: A large enterprise with complex hybrid cloud, on-premises, and Kubernetes environments wants AI-driven continuous resource optimization that maintains performance SLAs while reducing infrastructure costs.
avoid_when: You need simple cost reporting (use lighter FinOps tools), lack automation governance for autonomous resource actions, or run only single-cloud without hybrid complexity.

auth.notes: OAuth2 via IBM Cloud IAM (Turbonomic acquired by IBM in 2022). Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped access. No webhooks. On-premises deployment option with local auth. IBM Turbonomic is now part of IBM Instana product family.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing via IBM; contact IBM sales
pricing.notes: Boston-based company. Founded 2009. Acquired by IBM in 2022 for $2.5B. Now IBM Turbonomic. Strong in enterprise hybrid cloud and Kubernetes optimization. Autonomous action execution unique differentiator (vs just recommendations). Proven ROI: typically 30-50% cloud cost reduction. VMware, AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes support.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v3 for entities, actions, markets, and reports. IBM IAM OAuth2. Limited public documentation — primarily for enterprise integration partners. IBM acquisition added documentation fragmentation. Turbonomic data model (entities, actions, markets) requires platform understanding. On-prem and SaaS deployment options.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Entity reads fully idempotent. Action reads idempotent. Market reads idempotent. Savings reads idempotent by time period.
known_agent_gotchas: IBM acquisition — authentication now uses IBM Cloud IAM for SaaS deployment
On-premises deployment has different auth than SaaS — clarify deployment type
Turbonomic data model (Supply Chain) requires platform understanding
Autonomous action execution is a production risk — implement action gating
API documentation fragmented across pre-IBM and post-IBM acquisition docs

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP compliant. US and EU data residency. IBM Cloud IAM OAuth2. Autonomous resource actions are privileged operations — implement change management controls. IBM acquisition brings enterprise security standards. Infrastructure access credentials stored by Turbonomic.
